D7 Print L5 Evaluate Reference

The Yule-Nielsen n-Factor

add one exponent for light scattering in the paper and the measured dot area swings back from optical illusion to mechanical truth

Yule and Nielsen patched Murray-Davies with an empirical exponent n that models how far light spreads under the ink before it leaves the paper. n = 1 is Murray-Davies (no spread); larger n removes more optical gain. Tuned to the paper's true scatter, it recovers the real mechanical dot area.

The grey curve is the mechanical truth; the faint red is Murray-Davies (n = 1), overreading. Slide n upward and the gold Yule-Nielsen curve drops onto the truth — exactly when n matches the paper's scatter factor.
